old_claim.note: Particle Accelerators provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: A medical linear accelerator is a clinical radiation-therapy implementation of a linear particle accelerator.

source_shape.source_locator: AAPM Virtual Museum external-beam radiotherapy exhibit, first therapy linac section.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes the first therapy linac as an 8 MeV stationary machine built by Metropolitan Vickers, installed at Hammersmith Hospital, and used for the first patient in 1953.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a required accelerator edge because the medical device is not merely helped by accelerator science; it is a linear accelerator adapted for radiotherapy.

ontology_before: Medical linacs treated particle accelerators as a generic enabler.
ontology_after: Medical linacs now treat linear particle acceleration as the required device architecture.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: the particle-accelerator edge is required.
would_reject_if: The node were re-scoped to radiotherapy workflow software rather than the treatment machine.
A source showed medical linacs can exist without linear particle acceleration.
The graph removed the clinical radiotherapy context while strengthening the accelerator edge.
short_reason: A medical linac is a linear particle accelerator used for radiotherapy.
